Title: Add New User
Preconditions: Admin logged into backend ({{BACK_URL}})
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Security module at {{BACK_URL}}
[ACT] Click Add User
[ACT] Enter user details: username, email, password
[ACT] Select account type: Manager
[ACT] Click Save
Assertions:
[STATE] User is created successfully
[DOM] User appears in user list
[STATE] Account type is set correctly
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/users - verify user in response
[CONSOLE] No error logs on save
[NETWORK] POST /api/platform/security/users returns 200

Title: Account Lockout After Failed Login Attempts
Preconditions: Admin logged into Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a test user account exists with known credentials, account lockout policy is enabled (e.g. 5 failed attempts)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{BACK_URL}} login page
[ACT] Enter the test user email
[ACT] Enter an incorrect password 5 times consecutively
[ASSERT] Observe the response after the 5th failed attempt
[ACT] Attempt to log in with the correct password
[ASSERT] Verify the account is locked
Assertions:
[STATE] After 5 failed attempts, system displays an account lockout message
[STATE] Correct credentials are rejected while account is locked
[DOM] Lockout message explains the situation
[ADMIN] Lockout is recorded in platform audit logs
[STATE] Successful login is prevented until account is unlocked
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] GET {{BACK_URL}}/api/platform/security/users/{id} - verify isLockedOut=true after lockout
[ADMIN] Audit log contains lockout event with timestamp and user
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions during lockout
Failure_Signals: Lockout not triggered after threshold; correct credentials accepted despite lockout; audit log missing lockout event

Title: Session Termination on Admin Lock
Preconditions: Admin logged into Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a second browser session is open as the test user
Steps:
[NAV] Open a browser session as a test user and navigate to {{BACK_URL}}
[ACT] As admin in a separate session, navigate to Security module > Users
[ACT] Open the test user account
[ACT] Lock the account (or disable it)
[NAV] Switch to the test user browser session
[ACT] Attempt to perform any action or navigate
[ASSERT] Observe session behavior
Assertions:
[STATE] Active session of the locked user is terminated or invalidated
[NAV] Test user session is redirected to the login page
[DOM] Error or notification message indicates the account has been locked
[STATE] The user cannot continue performing actions after account lock
[ADMIN] Session termination is reflected in audit logs
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] Verify test user's session token is invalidated (401 on any authenticated request)
[ADMIN] Audit log contains session termination event
[CONSOLE] No unhandled server errors
Failure_Signals: Active session not terminated after account lock; user can continue actions after lock; no redirect to login

Title: Session Termination on Account Deletion

[ADMIN] Deletion is recorded in the platform audit log

Title: Authorization Scopes
Preconditions: Admin logged into Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a role with restricted permissions (e.g. catalog:read only) is created and assigned to a test user

[ACT] Attempt to create a new catalog item (should be blocked without catalog:create)
[ACT] Attempt to delete a catalog item (should be blocked without catalog:delete)
[ASSERT] Observe the UI feedback for blocked actions
[ACT] Log out and verify admin can perform all operations
Assertions:
[STATE] User with catalog:read scope can view catalog items
[DOM] Create button is hidden or disabled for user without catalog:create permission
[DOM] Delete button is hidden or disabled without catalog:delete permission
[API] API calls for restricted operations return 403 Forbidden
[STATE] No privilege escalation is possible through the UI

[API] DELETE {{BACK_URL}}/api/catalog/catalogs/{id} - expect 403 for restricted user
[NETWORK] 403 responses for unauthorized operations
[CONSOLE] No unhandled exceptions
Failure_Signals: Restricted user can create or delete catalog items; 200 returned for blocked operations; UI shows enabled buttons for disallowed actions

Title: Zero-Price and Negative Price Prevention
Preconditions: Admin logged into Backend ({{BACK_URL}}), a product with pricing enabled exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to Catalog module at {{BACK_URL}} and open a product with an existing price
[NAV] Navigate to the Prices tab or Pricing section
[ACT] Attempt to enter a negative value: -10.00 in the price field
[ACT] Attempt to save
[ASSERT] Observe validation behavior
[ACT] Attempt to enter 0.00 as the price
[ACT] Attempt to save and observe behavior
Assertions:
[STATE] Negative price value triggers a validation error
[DOM] Error message states that price must be zero or greater (or greater than zero depending on policy)
[STATE] Product does not save with a negative price
[STATE] Zero price behavior follows configured policy (allowed or blocked)
[STATE] Positive price values save normally without errors
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] POST/PUT price with negative value - expect 400 validation error
[API] POST/PUT price with 0.00 - verify policy-compliant response
[CONSOLE] Validation error for negative price
[NETWORK] 400 response for negative price save
Failure_Signals: Negative price accepted without error; product saves with -10.00 price; no validation message shown; zero price treated inconsistently
Cleanup: restore original product price via Admin
